intro: Echinocoryne stricta, also known as Desert Ironwood or Guayacan, is a small, deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Echinocoryne stricta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation in herbal medicine.
